summary.intent: Implement Task #286 — the framework-agnostic state machine core for the Table data-display component in the ars-ui Rust library, including all variants (SelectAll, Column Resizing, Virtual Scrolling ARIA slice, Async loading) and mandatory spec drift fixes.
summary.outcome: The Table agnostic core was fully implemented and audited: 1843-line mod.rs, 1990-line tests.rs with 94 unit/snapshot tests, spec conformance, proptest invariants, and doc test. All 11 spec drifts were fixed in the same PR. Zero clippy warnings, 99.4% line coverage, 1595 lib tests passing. Commit created and PR opened to GitHub with Closes #286.

repo: Tabs (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/mod.rs`) is the canonical structural template for complex components — section order, derive set, TransitionPlan style, and SyncProps/SyncControlledValue naming all mirror it.
`type Effect = NoEffect` is the correct choice for components that delegate all DOM side-effects (focus, scroll) to adapters; adapters read focused_cell/focused_row/focused_col directly from Context.
The workspace convention marks slow proptests `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly extended-proptest job"]` — they run only on the nightly extended-proptest CI job.
`cargo insta test --features i18n --unreferenced=reject` is the correct command to verify no orphaned snapshot fixtures.
Spec drift must be fixed in the same task/PR (CLAUDE.md rule) — spec edits land alongside code, not in a follow-up.
`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate (10 steps); must pass before any push.
The post-implementation-audit skill is mandatory before user review per CLAUDE.md step 7 — it runs spec/impl drift, iterative missing-anything, and test-coverage phases.
`ars_collections::selection::State::toggle` requires a `Collection` generic that the Table machine doesn't own — toggles must be computed locally via `is_selected` + `select`/`deselect`.
`selection::Set` is `#[non_exhaustive]`, so match arms need a `_` fallback even when all known variants are covered.
The GitHub Project board uses `gh project item-edit` with a `--field-id` + `--single-select-option-id` to move issues between status columns.
Line coverage threshold for ars-components is 90%; branch coverage threshold also applies and must be checked separately.
`Direction::Auto` must be resolved to a concrete direction in `init` from `env.locale` — it cannot remain as `Auto` in Context at runtime.

code.finding: Single-file module ~1843 LOC following tabs section order: State → Event → EscapeKeyBehavior → SelectAllMode → Messages → Props → Context → Part → Machine → Api. Uses `type Effect = NoEffect` and `TransitionPlan::context_only` for all transitions.
Closure type inference failures occur with `context_only` when the closure captures variables of ambiguous type — annotating the closure parameter `|ctx: &mut Context|` resolves it.

`SyncControlledSortDescriptor(Option<SortDescriptor<String>>)` requires wrapping the payload in `Some(...)` inside the transition because the outer Option on Bindable::sync_controlled is the controlled-mode toggle, not the value itself.

Reference implementation for complex components: shows correct pattern for Bindable controlled/uncontrolled fields, SyncProps + SyncControlledValue event naming, Machine::transition arm structure, and ConnectApi::part_attrs dispatcher.
§1.7 FocusCell hard-coded row 0 in the spec (literal bug). Real payload must carry `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }` and write `(col, row_index)` to focused_cell.

§3.4.3 build_sort_announcement referenced undeclared `locale` and used `SortDescriptor::column_key` (real field is `column`); must be marked `rust,no_check` until the adapter helper is in scope for spec-snippet compilation.
§1.1.1 mistakenly mentioned a `Scrolling` state — the single-`Idle` design is correct; sort state lives in Context, scroll lives in adapters.
Proptest module required a closing brace for the table proptest module that was missing after the append; always verify brace balance when appending to existing test files.

workflow: Write tests first (TDD), get them failing, then implement — the plan explicitly separated test-writing (Task 4) from implementation (Task 3) even though both files were written in the same session.

The post-implementation-audit skill runs three phases: (1) spec/impl drift table, (2) iterative 'anything else missing?' rounds until two consecutive clean rounds, (3) coverage audit with a quantitative threshold check.
Mutation testing with cargo-mutants on a large module (~195 mutants) takes ~30 min for a full run — run a representative shard (--shard 0/8) during the audit to get signal without blocking.
Coverage gaps are best targeted by examining mutation-testing output, not just line coverage — surviving mutants reveal which branches lack assertions even when lines are covered.
`cargo clippy --fix` cleans up `unused_mut`, fully-qualified `std::string::ToString::to_string`, and other mechanical warnings automatically.
Socket connection drops mid-response (API errors) don't lose work if tools already ran — the plan write simply needed to be retried.
Spec adapter specs (leptos-components/, dioxus-components/) must be updated in the same PR when the agnostic API signatures change, otherwise adapter authors get stale method signatures.

friction: API socket connection closed unexpectedly mid-response while writing the plan, requiring a retry with a separate Write tool call.

Eleven spec drifts required fixing before implementation could be considered complete — the spec was internally inconsistent across multiple sections (§1.1, §1.2, §1.3, §1.5, §1.7, §1.8, §3.4.2, §3.4.3, §4).
selection::State::toggle requires a Collection generic the Table machine doesn't own, requiring a local workaround (is_selected + select/deselect) instead of calling toggle directly.
Initial branch coverage came in below the 90% threshold, requiring two rounds of additional tests to reach 85.99% then exceed it.
Closure type inference failures with context_only required explicit parameter type annotations on multiple transition arms.
The proptest module was missing a closing brace after appending, causing a compile error that required a follow-up fix.

Adapter specs (leptos-components/, dioxus-components/) referenced stale API signatures (wrong arity for column_header_attrs, cell_attrs, select_all_attrs, column_resize_handle_attrs) and needed separate fixes.
The snapshot review step required running cargo insta test with the i18n feature flag explicitly, otherwise some fixtures were not exercised.
open_items: ScrollToRow(Key) event is deferred to a follow-up issue — implementing it requires widening Effect from NoEffect to a typed enum and pulling in ars-collections::Virtualizer plumbing.
Full Virtualizer integration and scroll-position management are deferred — this PR only adds ARIA-slice virtual scrolling (aria-rowcount, aria-colcount, aria-rowindex).
BulkSelection exclusion-set wrapper is excluded from the agnostic core — it is adapter/application code that wraps selection::Set::All.
on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>> callback is adapter-only and not stored in the core — deferred to the adapter layer.
Full mutation-testing run (195 mutants, ~30 min) was not completed — only a representative shard was run during the audit.
The PR requires the snapshot-reviewed label before merge (per workspace convention, since it introduces 37 new .snap files).
Codex review wait (waiting-for-codex-review label) is mandatory after the first push per CLAUDE.md step 12.
